hypothesis.statement: CO spillover from Au domains to Cu domains is the primary mechanism driving C2H4 selectivity enhancement in Cu-Au patterned electrodes, with selectivity scaling inversely with Au-to-Cu domain spacing at spacings below the CO diffusion length (~80 um).

scope.conditions_summary: Patterned Cu-Au electrodes in flow cell, -0.8 to -1.2V RHE, 1M KHCO3

generalizable_to.basis: Ag has similarly weak CO binding (E_ads ~ +0.01 eV on Ag(111)), making it an effective CO source like Au
Pd binds CO more strongly than Au (E_ads ~ -0.60 eV), so CO desorption and spillover may be kinetically limited

source_details.query: Cu-Au CO2 reduction C-C coupling selectivity mechanism

reasoning_chain.statement: Au selectively reduces CO2 to CO with high faradaic efficiency (>90%) due to weak CO binding (E_ads ~ -0.01 eV on Au(111))
Cu binds CO moderately (E_ads ~ -0.42 eV on Cu(111)), enabling subsequent C-C coupling to C2+ products
In Cu-Au patterned electrodes, CO produced on Au domains can diffuse to nearby Cu domains, increasing local CO coverage
Higher local CO coverage on Cu promotes C-C coupling (CO* + CO* -> OCCO*), which is the rate-limiting step for C2H4 formation
Geometry data confirms: 80um Cu / 20um Au striped electrode achieves 25.8% FE(C2H4) vs <1% on pure Cu, consistent with Cu domain size matching the CO diffusion length
reasoning_chain.reference: ISAAC records: Cu-Au CO2RR performance dataset

mechanism.description: Tandem catalysis: Au domains selectively produce CO from CO2, which spills over to adjacent Cu domains where elevated CO* coverage promotes C-C coupling via the OCCO* intermediate pathway to C2H4.
mechanism.rate_limiting_step: CO* + CO* -> OCCO* (C-C coupling on Cu)

length_scale.description: CO diffusion distance from Au to Cu domains under reaction conditions

predictions.magnitude: >10x increase in FE(C2H4) at optimal geometry

conditions.geometry: Cu stripe width 60-100 um, Au stripe width 15-25 um

predictions.falsification_criterion: If Cu-Au patterned electrodes show no C2H4 enhancement over pure Cu at any geometry, the spillover mechanism is falsified

predictions.magnitude: Maximum FE(C2H4) at Cu stripe width ~ CO diffusion length (~80 um)
predictions.falsification_criterion: If FE(C2H4) increases monotonically with Cu fraction (no optimum at a characteristic length scale), the diffusion-length argument is wrong

predictions.magnitude: CO FE should decrease as Cu-to-Au spacing decreases (more CO consumed by Cu)
predictions.falsification_criterion: If FE(CO) remains unchanged in Cu-Au patterned electrodes vs pure Au, CO is not transferring to Cu

predictions.magnitude: CO binding on Cu sites near Au should differ by <0.1 eV (electronic effect is secondary to transport)
predictions.falsification_criterion: If E_ads(CO) on Cu near Au differs by >0.3 eV from pure Cu(111), electronic modification (not spillover) is the dominant effect

confidence.basis: Strong geometry-dependent experimental trend consistent with diffusion-limited spillover. UMA data shows expected binding energy trends (weak CO on Au, moderate on Cu). No direct CO transport measurement yet.

descriptors.name: hypothesis_status
descriptors.definition: Current hypothesis status after evidence evaluation. Vocabulary: proposed, supported, contradicted, eliminated, needs_more_data, superseded.

descriptors.name: hypothesis_score
descriptors.definition: Net evidence score: +1 per supporting observation, -1 per contradicting observation, 0 per neutral observation.

links.notes: Hypothesis derived from geometry-dependent C2H4 selectivity in Cu-Au CO2RR performance evidence record
